body_txt: Characters that are wild, brutal, and savage often tend to be associated with wild, brutal, and savage weapons . Any kind of weapon that requires a modicum of Super-Strength to effectively wield tends to be in the hands of people who forsake any kind of subtlety and simply wish to destroy their opponents with a level of sheer force that can only be described as overkill , though they may eschew and view with scorn the large clubs or spiked maces in favor of the classic battleaxe. These characters often tend to be hot-headed and impetuous, and tend to usually have a strategy of endless attacking until the enemy is dead . Broadly speaking, axes tend to be strongly associated with Barbarian Tribes and savage, warlike people, particularly the broadly pseudo-Celtic and pseudo-Germanic barbarians of fantasy. Barbarian Heroes and wild berserkers are thus often depicted as favoring huge, heavy, notched battleaxes that they wield with savage, powerful swings. Crude and heavy axes are also very commonly the weapons of choice for "savage" fantasy races, such as orcs , ogres , beast men , and the like. Minotaurs are also strongly associated with axes, from both the savage demeanors they are usually given, and the use of double-headed axes as a decorative motif of the Cretan palaces from which the legend of the Minotaur came. While Dwarves are typically a honor-bound Proud Warrior and Industrious Race , their axe-based Fantastic Race Weapon Affinity can also overlap here when they produce fierce warriors and berserkers. Outside of broader cultural groups and tribes, individual axe-wielding characters still tend to be portrayed as being barbarous, wild, unruly and aggressive, living on the fringes of society and with Hot-Blooded demeanors. You'll rarely find royalty or other "refined" characters specialized in axe-work unless they are the king of a Barbarian Tribe or kicked asses on their way to being crowned such . Historically, this trope came about due to axes being a relatively simple weapon to develop, thanks to their origin as working tools, and as such of their being frequently made among tribal or other "savage" communities. In particular, battleaxes were common weapons among the Germanic peoples that overran the Western Roman Empire (particularly the Franks, who gave their name to the "francisca", a type of throwing axe) and later the Norse , leading them to becoming associated with barbaric peoples and raiders in European culture. Later, during the colonial age, the use of tomahawks among Native Americans likely added to the stereotype. Due to the mass exportation of Western European culture in the last two hundred years, this has become a common trope in media of other cultures that had no real history with this stereotype, such as Eastern RPGs in Japan. If the battleaxe wielded by these characters needs to be more elaborate, it can literally receive an extra edge, and be designed with a double-bladed head for a more badass look. While "double-bitted" axes are common as woodcutting tools, they're much more Awesome, but Impractical for fighting: the extra mass in the head makes them unbalanced weapons, and now the wielder has to make sure to not chop their face off with one of the blades. None of the real-world inspirations for fictional barbarians commonly used double-bladed battleaxes, despite how popular they are in fiction... But it can imply the wielder is just that deadly of a warrior, even with an inefficient weapon. Do keep in mind that merely using an axe as a weapon or being The Big Guy doesn't necessarily get them associated with this trope. The character has to be barbarous in either culture , fighting style or temperament to qualify for this trope; that is, the broadly pseudo -German, pseudo-Norse, or pseudo-Native-American wandering barbarian or savage northern/forest/mountain barbaric tribe of fantasy. Subtrope of Brutish Character, Brutish Weapon . In the case of the more evil brutish sorts, this can overlap with Good Weapon, Evil Weapon . Has overlap with Heavy Equipment Class . See also Clothing Reflects Personality , Fantastic Race Weapon Affinity , Barbarian Hero , Carry a Big Stick , Primitive Clubs , and Savage Spiked Weapons . When this gets made into a sword, see Sinister Scimitar . Contrast Disciplined Spearman .
